mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 65% of triggered months or the t-statistic for return difference versus baseline is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized household demand recovery, which directly lifts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample OLS or joint positive signals yield negative median forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index drop below its 6-month moving average signals immediate supply-side contraction, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 70% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low policy rates combined with elevated unemployment create a liquidity trap environment where monetary easing fails to stimulate growth, resulting in negative equity returns due to stagnant earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Joint condition yields positive median 20-day returns or interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in quantile regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 60% of above-threshold GDP releases or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 in a single month demonstrate robust labor demand, increasing consumer income velocity and driving positive equity market momentum over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of strong payroll months or signal accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month increases in the consumer price index above 0.4 percent trigger immediate inflation hedging behavior and anticipated policy tightening, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of high-inflation months or the signal fails to predict returns when CPI rises above threshold.
